name: Carrot-Pumpkin Bars with Orange Icing
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 15x10x1 inch baking pan and set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, orange peel, baking soda, and salt. Set the flour mixture aside.
3. In a medium bowl, combine eggs and brown sugar. Stir in pumpkin, oil, milk, and vanilla. Then stir in carrots and walnuts.
4. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture, stirring with a wooden spoon until combined. Spread batter into the prepared pan.
5. Bake in a 350°F oven for 20-25 minutes or until a wooden toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean.
6. Cool in the pan on a wire rack. Spread with Orange Icing and cut into triangles or bars.
7. If desired, garnish each with a walnut half. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Makes 36.
ORANGE ICING:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups sifted powdered sugar and enough orange liqueur or orange juice (1-2 tablespoons) to make an icing that is easy to drizzle.
